title: [ImportVerilog] Add case statement.
body:
Use `scf.if` and `moore.or` to implement the selection logic for case statements. For example, if a default case exists, it will perform an OR operation on all the results above, to determine whether the default statement needs to be executed. If the result of the operation is zero, the default statement will be executed.
However, this still leaves some issues, according to the "IEEE Standard 1800-2017 for SystemVerilog", which mentions that "During the linear search, if one of the Case_item_expressions matches the case_expression, then the statement associated with that case_item should be executed, and the linear search shall terminate." I want to know how we can implement this short-circuit logic?
